extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Contains one long low “valley” (values around 0.2–0.3) between two medium plateaus (values around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: Has a single sharp, brief “peak” (values above the surrounding plateau, around 0.7–0.8) that appears just after the low valley ends.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Contains one long high “plateau” (values around 0.8) between two lower regions (values around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: Outside the plateau, the series stays roughly flat and noisy without any sharp peaks or deep valleys.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Contains one long low “valley” (values around 0.15–0.2) between two medium plateaus (values around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: Has a very deep, narrow “dip” (values dropping well below the surrounding plateau) near the end of the series, while the rest of the post‑valley region returns to the medium plateau level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: For most of the middle segment, the signal dips clearly below its usual level and stays low (a long “valley”).
R2: After this valley, the signal briefly rises into a sharp “spike” that is higher than the initial level, then returns near the initial level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts and ends around the same moderate level.
R2: Between these ends, there is one long, wide “plateau” where the signal is consistently and clearly higher than the start/end level (a sustained elevated block).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: For most of the middle segment, the signal dips clearly below its initial level and stays low (a long “valley” similar to class 0).
R2: Within or near the end of this low region, there is an additional deep, narrow dip that goes even lower than the main valley (a small, very deep notch).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long, clearly depressed plateau in the middle (values drop well below the surrounding baseline and stay low for many time steps).
R2: After this depression, there is a single, short spike that rises noticeably above the baseline before returning to near‑baseline levels.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long, clearly elevated plateau in the middle (values rise above the surrounding baseline and stay high for many time steps).
R2: Outside this central high plateau (at the beginning and end), values remain near a common, lower baseline with no large spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long, clearly depressed plateau in the middle (values drop well below the surrounding baseline and stay low for many time steps).
R2: In the latter part of the series, there is an additional sharp, deep dip that goes even lower than the depressed plateau and then returns to the original baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Has a long low-valued “valley” in the middle of the series, with values clearly below 0.3.  
R2: After the valley, rises back to around 0.5 and contains a single short “spike” above 0.7.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Has a long high-valued “plateau” in the middle of the series, with values clearly above 0.7.  
R2: Before and after the plateau, values stay around 0.48–0.5 without deep drops below 0.45.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Has a long low-valued “valley” in the middle of the series, with values clearly below 0.3.  
R2: In the latter part of the series, contains a deep, narrow “dip” below 0.2 that interrupts otherwise higher values around 0.48–0.5.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Contains a long central low plateau (middle section of the series clearly lower than the beginning and end).
R2: Contains a single pronounced positive spike near the right side, rising clearly above the surrounding high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Contains a long central high plateau (middle section clearly higher than the beginning and end).
R2: No strong isolated spikes; the series rises smoothly into the high plateau and falls smoothly back.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Contains a long central low plateau (middle section clearly lower than the beginning and end).
R2: Contains a strong deep negative dip near the right side, clearly lower than both the earlier low plateau and the final values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series stays around a mid‑level value (~0.45–0.5) most of the time, drops once to a clearly lower plateau (~0.2–0.25) around the middle, then returns to the mid‑level.
R2: After returning from the low plateau, there is a single short, pronounced peak higher than all previous values (~0.75–0.8) before settling again to the mid‑level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a similar lower level (~0.47–0.5).
R2: There is one long, wide plateau at a higher nearly constant level (~0.8–0.82) in the middle, with no deep dips or sharp spikes elsewhere.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series alternates between a high level (~0.47–0.5) and a low level (~0.15–0.2), spending long stretches at each.
R2: Within the second high segment (after a long low plateau), there is a single very deep, narrow dip that goes noticeably below the preceding low plateau before returning to the high level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long low “valley” in the middle (values clearly lower than both start and end levels).
R2: Near the end, there is a short, sharp positive spike that rises above the surrounding high level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a lower level and has one wide, flat “plateau” in the middle at a clearly higher value.
R2: Outside this plateau, the level is roughly constant and no additional large spikes or dips appear.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long low “valley” in the middle, between two higher outer segments.
R2: Within or near the right half, there is an extra narrow, deep dip that goes below the main valley before returning to the higher outer level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal starts and ends at a medium level, dips to a clearly lower plateau in the middle, then returns to the medium level.  
R2: Near the end of the series there is a single, narrow spike that rises clearly above the surrounding medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts at a lower level, rises quickly to a higher plateau around the middle, then drops quickly back to the lower level.  
R2: During that middle section the signal stays high and roughly flat, without deep dips or sharp spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal starts and ends at a medium level, with a long, clear low plateau in the middle of the series.  
R2: Within or near the end of that low region there are one or more narrow, deep drops that go significantly below the low plateau before returning to it or to the medium level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series stays around a medium level, drops to a clearly lower plateau for some time, then returns to the medium level.  
R2: Near the end of the series, there is a single sharp peak that rises clearly above all previous values.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins at a medium level, then rises quickly to a higher plateau that stays high for a long central segment, and finally falls back to the original medium level.  
R2: There are no sharp isolated peaks or dips; the main pattern is one wide, flat “high block” in the middle.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series begins at a medium level, then falls quickly to a clearly lower plateau for a long central segment, and later returns to the medium level.  
R2: After returning to the medium level, there is a very deep and narrow dip near the end that goes clearly below all previous values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the initial level) and then returns to the initial level.
R2: During the return to the initial level, there is a single short, pronounced positive peak higher than the initial level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the initial level) and then returns to approximately the initial level.
R2: Outside this high plateau (start and end segments), the values stay roughly constant and similar to each other.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the initial level) between two higher regions.
R2: In the second higher region (after the low plateau), there is a sharp deep dip (a short valley) that goes significantly below that region’s surrounding level.
